# Proof of Every locally convex TVS topology is generated by its continuous seminorms

Let $(E, \tau)$ be a locally convex TVS over $\mathbb{K}$, and let $\mathcal{F}$ be the set of all continuous seminorms on $E$.

By (37.5), $\tau_p \subset \tau$ for every $p \in \mathcal{F}$, thus $\tau(\mathcal{F}) \subset \tau$.

Conversely, let $V$ be any closed, balanced, convex neighborhood of $\theta$ for the topology $\tau$. By (37.4), there exists $p \in \mathcal{F}$ such that $V = \{x : p(x) \leq 1\}$ and the $\tau$-interior of $V$ is $\{x : p(x) < 1\} \in \tau_p \subset \tau(\mathcal{F})$. Thus $V$ is a neighborhood of $\theta$ for $\tau(\mathcal{F})$. Since the closed, balanced, convex neighborhoods form a fundamental system of neighborhoods of $\theta$ for $\tau$ (by definition of local convexity), it follows that $\tau \subset \tau(\mathcal{F})$.

Therefore $\tau = \tau(\mathcal{F})$.
